eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket's trader consensus prices a nail-biter for UK Q1 2026 GDP growth, with the 0.6-0.9% bucket edging out 0.3-0.6% at 51.3% versus 48.1% implied probabilities, while 1.2-1.5% trails closely at 47.9%, highlighting divided views on quarterly momentum ahead of the ONS preliminary release on May 14. February's stronger-than-expected 0.5% monthly GDP expansion—driven by 0.5% services and 1.2% production gains—lifted three-month growth to 0.5%, offsetting January's mere 0.1% rise, with April's flash composite PMI rebound to 52.0 signaling March resilience amid manufacturing surge to 53.6. Yet persistent CPI inflation at 3.3% y/y, steady 4.9% unemployment, and geopolitical headwinds like the Iran conflict cap upside, leaving construction weakness as a key swing factor.

markets.question: Nord Stream pipeline turned on before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any line of either Nord Stream 1 or Nord Stream 2 delivers natural gas to Germany or any EU member state in commercial quantities at any point by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Commercial quantities" refers to sustained, measurable flows of gas intended for end-user distribution. Test flows, pressure tests, or symbolic gas transfers not tied to actual supply operations will not count.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 93.5% chance against the Nord Stream pipelines resuming operations before 2027, anchored by Germany's repeated official rejections of any restart, including Chancellor Friedrich Merz's May 2025 pledge to block revival amid EU sanctions and energy diversification to LNG terminals. Despite Russian claims of an intact pipe ready for quick activation and unverified reports of early 2026 US-Russia talks on US-led reopening under a potential Trump-Putin deal, no repair permits have advanced, Nord Stream AG faces legal insolvency, and sabotage investigations from the 2022 explosions linger without resolution. Ongoing Ukraine conflict escalation signals and Baltic Sea territorial disputes further entrench barriers, with traders viewing concrete progress as improbable absent a broader diplomatic thaw.

events.title: Argentina vs. Algeria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Tuesday, June 16, 2026 between Argentina and Algeria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending World Cup champions Argentina lead trader consensus at 68.5% implied probability for their FIFA World Cup Group J opener against Algeria on June 17 at Arrowhead Stadium, fueled by superior FIFA ranking, Lionel Messi's enduring influence, and unmatched squad depth despite recent injuries. Key defender Cuti Romero's Achilles rupture (announced within the last day) and striker Lautaro Martínez's recurring calf strain sidelined him for weeks, softening odds from prior peaks but not eroding the talent gap. Algeria, at 11.5%, draws respect for coach Vladimir Petković's tactical setup—praised by Lionel Scaloni—and recent Africa Cup of Nations form, yet faces historical deficit (sole prior meeting: 4-3 Argentina friendly win in 2007). Draw pricing at 19% reflects group-stage caution in neutral-site matchup amid Algeria's own absences like goalkeeper Anthony Mandréa (shoulder).

markets.question: Will Kaitlin Purrington be the Republican nominee for AZ-01?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the AZ-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on August 4, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jay Feely's trader consensus lead in the AZ-01 Republican primary stems from high-profile endorsements, including President Trump's early backing, House Republican leadership, NRCC inclusion in its MAGA Majority program, and incumbent Rep. Juan Ciscomani's support announced April 28. These signals of party infrastructure momentum have elevated his implied probability despite a NextGen poll released April 21 showing Joseph Chaplik ahead 24%-15% in ballot tests among likely voters, with 54% undecided. Chaplik, a former state representative who resigned in February to focus on the race, draws support from his legislative record and conservative credentials, positioning him as the main challenger in this open battleground district ahead of the August primary. High undecideds leave room for fundraising, events like Feely's recent RFK Jr. rally, and further endorsements to shift dynamics.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Pokrovsk by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Pokrovsk, Donetsk Oblast, (48.303080° N, 37.192568° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Pokrovsk will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of the specified area, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/rwDQehLsZAUsquVX7

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue high-tempo assaults and small-unit infiltrations northwest and southwest of Pokrovsk in Donetsk Oblast, but ISW assessments on April 27-28 confirm no advances amid Ukrainian repels by the 7th Rapid Reaction Corps using drone strikes and artillery. Logistical strains limit Russian supply to forward troops, while Ukrainian forces hold northern outskirts and conduct counterpressure near Hryshyne and Rodynske. Following a reported partial seizure of Pokrovsk in late February after two years of fighting, full control remains elusive per geolocated footage and frontline clashes exceeding 100 daily in the sector. Traders watch ISW maps for resolution criteria, with U.S. aid flows and seasonal weather as potential catalysts for shifts.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 for the week of May 2, 2026, fueled by massive streaming and sales from her latest single release, solidifying her as the newest chart-topper this year. Joining 2026 #1 artists like Ella Langley (seven non-consecutive weeks with "Choosin' Texas," currently #2), Taylor Swift (two songs), Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, Bad Bunny, BTS, and a Mariah Carey holiday re-entry, the market reflects strong country-pop crossovers and K-pop momentum amid shifting radio airplay and platform algorithms. With eight months left, traders eye May album drops from Chris Brown and others, plus weekly tracking updates, as debut strategies and viral TikTok traction could propel fresh contenders like Sabrina Carpenter or Post Malone to the summit.

markets.question: LoL: LUA Gaming vs Barça eSports - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between LUA Gaming and Barça eSports in the LES Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "LUA Gaming" if LUA Gaming win Game 1 against Barça eSports.

This market will resolve to "Barça eSports" if Barça eSports win Game 1 against LUA Gaming.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: LUA Gaming vs Barça eSports (BO3) - LES Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between LUA Gaming and Barça eSports in the LES Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 30 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "LUA Gaming" if LUA Gaming win the match against Barça eSports.

This market will resolve to "Barça eSports" if Barça eSports win the match against LUA Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barça eSports sits third in LES 2026 Spring regular season standings at 2-1 (5-3 games), bolstered by a recent 2-1 win over UCAM Esports Club on April 22 and 2-0 sweep of UB Alma Mater on April 16, fueling their top-4 playoff push amid a two-series win streak. LUA Gaming, conversely, anchors the bottom at 0-3 (0-6 games), swept 0-2 by elite foes including Movistar KOI Fénix on April 23 and Heretics Academy earlier, exposing macro and execution woes in a winless skid. Head-to-head history tilts toward Barça, with stable rosters—Barça's Koldo jungle and Legolas ADC versus LUA's Pegaso and Kaii—setting up a pivotal BO3 where LUA seeks a breakout amid trash talk from ADC Kaii1.

markets.question: Will Sheffield Wednesday FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 2, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Sheffield Wednesday FC vs. West Bromwich Albion FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming EFL Championship game, scheduled for Saturday, May 2, 2026 between Sheffield Wednesday FC and West Bromwich Albion FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: West Bromwich Albion's 63% implied probability reflects trader consensus on their superior Championship standing (21st with 51 points after a recent two-point deduction) and unbeaten run in six matches (D-W-W-D-D-D), contrasting Sheffield Wednesday's bottom-table position (24th, already relegated in February with a record-early drop) and winless home form (0-7-15). Both sides grapple with injuries—Owls missing Iké Ugbo, Di'Shon Bernard, Liam Cooper, and others; Baggies without Josh Maja (ankle), Jed Wallace (doubtful leg), and Mikey Johnston (surgery)—yet West Brom's head-to-head edge (8 wins to Wednesday's 4 in recent H2H) and depth position them as clear favorites despite poor away record, with a draw at 20.5% viable in a low-stakes finale for the hosts.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Democrats a commanding 92.5% implied probability to hold Pennsylvania's 6th congressional district, driven by incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's bid for a fifth term in the reliably Democratic suburbs of Chester County. Houlahan's consistent wide-margin victories, including 2024, reflect the district's leftward shift and her cross-party appeal as a moderate veteran and business leader. With May 19 primaries approaching, no formidable Republican challengers have emerged to alter dynamics amid a thin GOP field. While odds exceed 90%, scenarios like a high-profile GOP recruit, Houlahan scandal, legal challenges, or a national midterm wave favoring the opposition party could narrow the gap before the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $435 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Microsoft (MSFT) shares closed at $429.25 on April 28, 2026, amid heightened volatility with a year-to-date decline of roughly 15% but recent monthly gains exceeding 10% as traders position ahead of fiscal Q3 earnings due after market close today. Azure cloud growth and AI investments remain core drivers, with consensus analyst estimates targeting EPS of $4.07 and average price targets near $570, implying significant upside potential. Options pricing suggests a 6% post-earnings move in either direction, positioning trader sentiment for a reaction on April 30's close—the market resolution date. Key focus: revenue beats in intelligent cloud segment versus elevated capital expenditures and competitive pressures from hyperscalers.

June 23 closed primaries will clarify nominees before the November 3 general election, with no recent general polls available but historical precedents favoring the GOP baseline.

markets.question: Anysphere (Cursor) IPO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for tech IPOs before 2027 hinges on recent signals from AI and space leaders like OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX, which have initiated early preparations including tender offers and governance tweaks, per New York Times reporting, amid a reopening IPO window buoyed by strong public market appetite for artificial intelligence and infrastructure plays. Cerebras Systems is poised to file its S-1 imminently, while Discord shows strong momentum from maturing revenue streams in gaming and enterprise communications. Competitive dynamics favor high-valuation unicorns like Databricks and Stripe, though volatility in tech valuations and potential regulatory scrutiny on AI firms introduce uncertainty. Key catalysts include upcoming S-1 filings and Q2 earnings from peers, with resolution tied to official exchange listings by year-end.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley as the frontrunner at 40.5% implied probability to serve as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated 2026 IPO—code-named Project Apex—due to its deep ties to Elon Musk, including banker Michael Grimes' role in Tesla's 2010 listing and recent return to the firm. Bank of America follows at 20.8%, bolstered by early January Financial Times reports naming it alongside Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan for senior underwriting roles. Recent catalysts include SpaceX's April 6 virtual meeting with 21 banks outlining a $75 billion raise at up to $1.75 trillion valuation, confirming those five as lead bookrunners, and April 24 bridge financing led by Goldman with the others participating. With no traditional "lead left" position and alphabetical listing likely, jockeying between Morgan Stanley and Goldman persists ahead of the late-May prospectus release and June 8 roadshow.

markets.question: Le Mans FC vs. Stade de Reims: O/U 2.5

This market will resolve to "Over" if Le Mans FC and Stade de Reims combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue2.fr. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Le Mans FC vs. Stade de Reims - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 2 game, scheduled for May 2 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Le Mans FC, entrenched in third place on the Ligue 2 table with 15 wins and robust home form (eight victories at Stade Marie-Marvingt), host fourth-placed Stade de Reims on May 2 in a pivotal promotion six-pointer. Le Mans boast momentum from recent triumphs including a 4-0 rout of Pau FC on April 6 and a 4-3 comeback at Amiens, bolstering their clean-sheet streak. Reims remain competitive but stumbled to a 2-2 draw at Laval last week, compounded by off-field disruption from armed fan threats at their training ground five days ago potentially impacting morale. Reims dominate head-to-head (unbeaten in six, including February's 3-0 Coupe de France win), though Le Mans contend without long-term absentee Jean Vercruysse (cruciate ligament tear).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's (HKO) latest forecast update at 11:30 HKT on April 29 projects a maximum temperature of 25°C for April 30 under mainly cloudy skies with east-northeast winds at force 4-5, driving the market-implied 75.5% probability for 25°C or higher as traders align with this official guidance backed by multi-model consensus. Current observations show morning temperatures at 23°C with 88% relative humidity, reflecting persistent moist conditions that could cap peak heating, while the 20.5% odds on exactly 24°C account for potential cloud-induced moderation. This fits 2026's above-normal seasonal temperature outlook and March's unseasonably warm pattern, though short-term forecast uncertainty remains; watch HKO's afternoon briefing for refined model runs amid stable subtropical high-pressure influences.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 200+ posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Ted Cruz's X posts from April 28 to May 5 reflects his established pattern of 10-15 daily outputs, with 60-79 and 100-119 bins deadlocked at 44.5% amid uncertainty over news-driven spikes. April 28 kicked off strongly with at least eight posts, featuring quote reactions to Ilhan Omar's World War II gaffe, NYC budget woes, EPA rescissions under Lee Zeldin, and SNAP fraud revelations, consistent with his high-engagement style blending policy critiques, memes, and cultural nods like Monty Python. The tight race persists due to variable catalysts—Senate floor fights or viral exchanges historically add 5-10 posts daily—against baselines from prior weeks' markets favoring 80-99 tallies. Absent major hearings or Trump administration announcements, steady commentary on midterms or foreign policy could tip toward higher bins, while quieter schedules favor the low 60s.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 9°C on May 1?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in short-range forecasts for Moscow's unusually cold May 1 high temperature, with market-implied odds tightly clustered at 37% for both 8°C and 9°C amid a broader 35-37% spread across 6-10°C outcomes—well below the historical average of 16°C. This positioning stems from a persistent Arctic air mass over European Russia, reinforced by recent northerly winds and precipitation as shown in the latest ECMWF and GFS model runs over the past 48 hours, which project highs of 7-9°C under overcast skies and light rain. Differentiating factors include ensemble variations in cloud cover timing and frontal passage speed: clearer afternoon scenarios favor 9-10°C, while prolonged overcast conditions tilt toward 6-7°C. New 00Z forecasts from NOAA and ECMWF, due within hours, could sharpen this divergence.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' W17 has dominated the early 2026 F1 season, powering George Russell and Kimi Antonelli to a 1-2 finish in Australia, with Antonelli securing victories in China and Japan, propelling them to the top of the Drivers' Championship standings at 72 and 63 points respectively ahead of the Miami Grand Prix. This team supremacy, underscored by a 135-90 constructors' lead over Ferrari, drives trader consensus with Russell's 41.5% implied probability reflecting his experience and qualifying edge over the surging rookie teammate, while Antonelli's 31.1% acknowledges his rapid ascent but highlights the intra-team battle's risks. Rivals like Verstappen (3.3%) lag due to Red Bull's uncompetitive start and strategic setbacks.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Mars" 2+ times during astronaut greeting?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to participate in an event titled "The President participates in a Greeting with Artemis II Astronauts" on April 29, 2026, 2PM ET (https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/calendar/). 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed term during the greeting with the Artemis II astronauts scheduled for April 29, 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about the event titled "The President participates in a Greeting with Artemis II Astronauts" scheduled for April 29, 2026, 2PM ET (https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/calendar/). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If this event is definitively cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by April 29, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, "-No Qualifying Event-" will resolve to "Yes" and all other brackets will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump is set to host the four Artemis II astronauts—Commander Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen—at the White House on Wednesday, days after NASA's Orion spacecraft completed its historic crewed lunar flyby, reaching a record 252,756 miles from Earth and validating life support and deep-space navigation systems for future landings. This first crewed Artemis mission since Apollo 17 advances NASA's goals for sustainable lunar presence via the Gateway station and eventual Mars missions, with official agency data confirming all systems performed nominally despite minor thermal shield anomalies under review. In a prior April 7 call, Trump praised the crew and vowed a permanent Moon base before Mars, informing trader sentiment on likely phrasing; NASA briefings this week may preview discussion topics amid Artemis III launch preparations slated for late 2027.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

Policymakers' recent statements highlight data-dependent easing, balancing persistent price pressures against labor market softening, with no immediate cuts signaled in March's summary of economic projections. Trader consensus via tools like CME FedWatch implies a shallow rate path into 2027, contingent on disinflation toward the 2% target. Watch April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC for pivot signals that could reshape the trajectory.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.
markets.groupItemTitle: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)

events.title: LoL: Anyone's Legend vs Weibo Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win against Weibo Gaming in the named LoL match in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Weibo Gaming" if Weibo Gaming win against Anyone's Legend in the LoL match.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anyone's Legend holds the edge in trader consensus for their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 against Weibo Gaming, riding a hot streak with 2-0 sweeps over Invictus Gaming and Ninjas in Pyjamas in the past week, propelling them into top-three standings at 5-3 series record. Weibo Gaming secured a morale-boosting 2-0 over Top Esports but sits precariously at 3-6 amid roster upheaval, losing jungler Tian and support Erha in late March. AL boasts a 57% head-to-head win rate, including a 2-0 earlier this split, with Tarzan's jungle pathing and macro sidelane pressure key factors. Top-four spots offer direct playoff berths, heightening stakes under Patch 26.7 meta shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus assigns a 98% implied probability to a high temperature of 64°F or higher in Seattle on April 30, driven by the National Weather Service forecast of sunny skies and highs in the mid-to-upper 60s amid a building upper-level ridge that suppresses typical onshore marine flow. Major models like GFS and ECMWF show tight agreement on clear conditions and light winds promoting efficient diurnal heating, a shift from cooler stratus-dominated patterns earlier in April when highs lingered in the 50s—well below the 57°F climatological average. markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump's tie be another color on April 29, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on the predominant color of the tie worn by Donald Trump during his first public appearance during which he wears a tie on the specified date (ET).

The color will be determined based solely on the first publicly available photo or video of the appearance. Reporting of the tie color without photos or videos will not be considered. Subsequent publication of other photos or videos, including those taken earlier, will not affect the resolution.

For the tie to be considered predominantly blue or red, its primary color must be a shade of blue or red, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. 

This tie would count as "Blue": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-blue-5ywRT5OXfn-5.jpg

This tie would count as "Red": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-red-a7ObxtpGQcrR.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "Other": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-other-Q8aIgOP7wFs1.jpg

If Donald Trump does not have any public appearance on the specified date, does not wear a tie, or if it is a color not listed among the strike options, the market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-other-Q8aIgOP7wFs1.jpg

If Donald Trump does not have any public appearance on the specified date, does not wear a tie, or if it is a color not listed among the strike options, the market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight competition between Red (30%) and Other (30%), with Blue trailing at 20%, driven by President Trump's signature power-red ties clashing against his recent diversification into unconventional shades like metallic purple—seen at February's Board of Peace meeting and yesterday's White House beehive tour with Melania's matching purple hat. The ongoing state visit by King Charles III and Queen Camilla elevates tonight's formal state dinner, where viral footage captures Trump in a crisp white bowtie, bolstering Other as a frontrunner for high-profile imagery. Daytime public appearances could still swing toward red, heightening uncertainty until confirmed photos resolve the market amid buzzing social media speculation.

markets.question: Will the Utah Mammoth win the Western Conference?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the Western Conference during the 2025-26 NHL Playoffs.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the Western Conference (e.g., elimination from the playoffs), the relevant market will resolve immediately to “No.”

If the 2025-26 NHL season is permanently canceled or the playoffs have not been completed by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche lead trader consensus at 49.5% implied probability to win the Western Conference after dominating the regular season with a 55-16-11 record and sweeping the Los Angeles Kings in four straight games, capped by a 5-1 victory on April 26 that advanced them to the second round while granting valuable rest ahead of facing the Minnesota Wild or Dallas Stars—both Central Division rivals they've handled well historically. Vegas Golden Knights (13.9%) hold firm in their Pacific Division matchup, splitting the first two games with Utah Mammoth (5.1%) in a tied series, leveraging home-ice advantage and defensive depth. Minnesota Wild (12.0%) surged back to even their first-round series 2-2 against Dallas Stars (6.5%) with a gritty 3-2 home win on April 27, fueling momentum in a high-stakes Game 5 tonight. Anaheim Ducks (6.0%) stun as wild-card upstarts leading Edmonton Oilers (7.0%) 3-1, driven by opportunistic scoring and goaltending, though sustaining against playoff-tested foes remains key amid the wisdom of crowds pricing in Avalanche depth and path advantages.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trader sentiment for hitting key levels in 2026 reflects persistent U.S. monetary policy divergence, with the Federal Reserve holding the federal funds rate at 3.50–3.75% post its April 28–29 meeting, outpacing the ECB's deposit facility at 2.00%. The pair traded around 1.1695–1.1700 on April 29, dipping below 1.1700 amid heightened Middle East geopolitical risks bolstering dollar safe-haven demand. ECB staff projections slashed Eurozone 2026 GDP growth to 0.9% from prior estimates, underscoring stagflation pressures and limiting euro upside. Upcoming catalysts include the ECB's April 30 decision and June FOMC meeting, alongside U.S. nonfarm payrolls and Eurozone inflation data, which could widen or narrow the 150-basis-point rate gap influencing exchange rate paths.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams leads trader consensus at 60% implied probability for Utah's 1st Congressional District Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his fundraising dominance—holding as much cash on hand as the rest of the field combined—name recognition from his 2019-2021 service, and a March Data for Progress poll showing 36% support among likely primary voters. Nate Blouin's 26.5% reflects progressive enthusiasm as a state senator criticizing McAdams' moderate record on issues like minimum wage and reproductive rights, amid recent attack ads and debates. At the April 26 Utah Democratic convention, McAdams captured 40% on the first ballot before losing ranked-choice endorsement to newcomer Liban Mohamed, but traders view the delegate-heavy event as left-skewed versus the broader primary electorate, positioning McAdams' base for a plurality win in the newly drawn blue-leaning district. Lower odds for Brian King, Caroline Gleich, and others highlight their limited polling and visibility.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting concludes today with markets pricing a hold at the 3.50%-3.75% federal funds target range, reflecting sticky March CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year and unemployment steady at 4.3% amid slowing job gains. Policymakers' recent statements highlight data-dependent easing, balancing persistent price pressures against labor market softening, with no immediate cuts signaled in March's summary of economic projections. Trader consensus via tools like CME FedWatch implies a shallow rate path into 2027, contingent on disinflation toward the 2% target. Watch April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC for pivot signals that could reshape the trajectory.

markets.question: Will DeepSeek have the #3 AI model at the end of April 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors Anthropic at 98.6% implied probability for holding the #3 AI model spot on the Arena.ai Text Arena leaderboard (Style Control On) as of April 29, 2026, driven by its Claude family dominating the top ranks: Claude Opus 4.7 Thinking at #1 (1503 Elo), Opus 4.6 Thinking at #2 (1502), and Opus 4.6 at #3 (1497)—a 4-point lead over #4 (also Anthropic) and 6 points over Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview at #5. Recent Anthropic releases, including Opus 4.7's strong showings in code and expert benchmarks from mid-April, have solidified this positioning amid crowdsourced battles exceeding millions of votes. With resolution at April 30 noon ET, realistic challenges include a last-minute OpenAI GPT-5.5 update, Google preview surge, or voting volatility shifting Elo scores by 5+ points.

markets.question: Will the median home value in Miami be less than $1,180,000 on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in Miami, Florida on April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket. 

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for Miami City. The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 2100, which is the median square footage in Miami. Parcl is set to publish this data on April 30, 2026. If no data for April 30 is released by May 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/23)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 82% implied probability to Miami's median home value remaining below $1.18 million on April 30, per the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for Miami City—recently tracking around $553 per square foot, equivalent to roughly $1.16 million using the 2,100 square foot median. This positioning reflects a softening trend from early April peaks near $1.23 million, driven by 21% year-over-year inventory growth signaling a balanced 6-month supply, elevated mortgage rates at 6-6.2%, and Redfin's March median sale price per square foot down 6.7% to $531. With resolution imminent tomorrow and no major catalysts, traders anticipate stability or mild further pressure absent demand surges.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "No" at 88% for the Elon Bull Run Parlay, reflecting deep skepticism that Elon Musk will achieve trillionaire status per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announce a 15th child, and oversee 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reaching 62-mile altitude—all in 2026. Tesla's Q1 earnings on April 24 beat EPS expectations at $0.41 but elicited a muted stock reaction amid softening EV demand and Chinese competition, leaving Musk's net worth around $800-850 billion despite February's SpaceX-xAI merger boosting valuations to $1.25 trillion combined. Starship Flight 12 faces May delays, underscoring cadence challenges with zero qualifying launches YTD. Absent credible reports of a new child, the parlay's multiplicative improbability dominates sentiment, though Q2 deliveries and potential SpaceX IPO rumors could shift odds.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' skin-in-the-game consensus prices South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation above 3.0% at a slim 51% implied probability, edging out the 2.1%-2.3% band at 49.1% following March's YoY rate uptick to 2.2%—driven by oil price surges from U.S.-Iran tensions—though it undershot forecasts at 2.4%. Bank of Korea's April 10 decision to hold the base rate at 2.5% reaffirmed a 2.0% full-year projection amid mid-to-high 2% signals, but OECD's upward revision to 2.7% and AMRO's 2.3% forecast underscore energy-driven upside risks versus potential demand cooling. The tight contest with <1.5% at 49% reflects bets on aggressive monetary tightening; April CPI data and May BoK board meeting loom as pivotal catalysts.
